export.title: Export
home.ctaSubtitle: No account required. No cloud. No strings.
home.encryptedDesc: Data is encrypted on your device before it goes anywhere. Only you hold the keys. Not even we can read what you write.
home.alwaysInSyncDesc: Edit on multiple devices. Go offline for days. Come back and everything merges — no conflicts, no data loss.
home.heroSubtitle: A canvas-based editor that runs on your device, syncs peer-to-peer, and answers to no server. Your data never leaves your hands.
home.localFirstDesc: Everything runs on your machine. Your data stored locally. No round-trips to servers, no loading spinners, no permission asked.
home.manifestoParagraph1: We built another editor. Not because the world needed one — but because every existing one asks you to trust someone else with your thoughts.
home.manifestoParagraph2: Tasfer is a rejection of that premise. No cloud you don't control. No server that can go down and take your work with it. No company that can read your notes, change the terms, or shut the doors.
home.manifestoParagraph3: Your documents live on your device. When you collaborate, data flows directly between peers — encrypted, ephemeral, yours. The architecture doesn't just support this philosophy. It enforces it.
home.peerToPeerDesc: Direct connections between devices. A thin relay finds your peers — then steps aside. No data stored, no data read, no data owned.
home.principle1Desc: Your editor should not have a login wall, a pricing page, or a terms of service update every quarter.
home.principle2Desc: Network is a feature, not a requirement. Everything works without it. Sync is a bonus.
home.principle3Desc: Open source means you can take this, change it, ship it. Build exactly what you want. No permission needed.

onboarding.createDesc: Start fresh with your own space
onboarding.identityDesc: Tasfer uses cryptographic keys instead of accounts.
onboarding.identityLocalDesc: Your private key never leaves this device. There is no server that stores your identity.
onboarding.identityUniqueDesc: A cryptographic keypair was generated on your device. It identifies you without accounts or passwords.
onboarding.identityVisibleDesc: Your name and avatar are shared only with people in your spaces. Nobody else can see them.

onboarding.profileDesc: Set up your profile. This is only visible to people you collaborate with in shared spaces.
onboarding.ruleMembers: Space members can see all pages in the space and edit them.

onboarding.ruleP2P: Everything syncs directly between devices — no cloud, no servers.
onboarding.rulePrivacy: Your data lives on your devices and syncs over a peer-to-peer connection.
onboarding.spaceDesc: A space is where your pages live. Create your own or join someone else's.
p2pTutorial.step1Desc: Right now this space lives only on your device. Inviting turns it into a shared space — you'll both edit the same notes, live.
p2pTutorial.relayCaption: a relay finds your peer — then steps aside
p2pTutorial.step2Desc: Tasfer connects you directly, over an encrypted link. A thin relay finds your peer, then steps aside — no server ever stores your notes.
p2pTutorial.step3Desc: Inviting replicates this entire space to their device. Archiving or leaving on your side deletes nothing from theirs — once it's sent, there's no recall.
p2pTutorial.confirmPermanence: I understand this can't be undone.

profile.setUpProfile: Set up your profile to get started
privacy.intro: Tasfer is built on a simple principle: your data is yours. We don't collect it, we don't store it, we don't want it.
privacy.noAnalyticsDesc: No cookies, no trackers, no telemetry. We don't know who you are, what you write, or how you use the app.
privacy.noCollectionDesc: Tasfer runs entirely on your device. We have no servers that receive, process, or store your content. Your documents never leave your machine unless you choose to sync with a peer.
privacy.noThirdPartyDesc: There is no data to share because there is no data to collect. No advertising, no partnerships, no data brokers.
privacy.openSourceDesc: Don't take our word for it. The entire codebase is open source — you can verify every claim on this page yourself.
privacy.p2pSyncDesc: When you collaborate, data flows directly between devices over encrypted WebRTC connections. Signaling runs on Cloudflare Workers and all signaling messages are end-to-end encrypted — the relay helps peers find each other but never sees your content.
privacy.title: privacy policy

install.title: Protect your notes
install.description: Right now your notes sit in browser storage, which the browser may clear to free up space. Here are a few ways to protect them.
install.descriptionMobile: In the browser, offline support is limited and your notes sit in storage the browser may clear. The app works fully offline and keeps notes in protected storage.
install.chromiumHelp: Click the highlighted <bold>install icon</bold> at the right end of the address bar, then choose <bold>Install</bold>.
install.safariHelp: Click <shareIcon /> <bold>Share</bold> in the toolbar, then choose <bold>Add to Dock</bold>.
install.iosHelp: Tap <shareIcon /> <bold>Share</bold> in the Safari toolbar, then <bold>Add to Home Screen</bold> <plusIcon />.
install.androidHelp: Tap the <moreIcon /> <bold>menu</bold> in Chrome, then <bold>Add to Home screen</bold> <addIcon />.
install.desktopIntro: The desktop app keeps your notes in its own storage, fully out of the browser's reach.
install.mobileIntro: Native apps for your phone, with the same notes synced across your devices.
storage.bannerDesc: The browser may clear it to free up space. Install Tasfer to keep them safe.
storage.bannerTitle: Your notes are in browser storage
storage.protectionDeclined: Your browser declined the request. Installing Tasfer as an app usually helps — then try again.
storage.protectionEnabled: Your local data is now protected from automatic cleanup.
storage.protectionOffDesc: Your browser may delete Tasfer's local data when disk space runs low. Protect it, or export backups regularly.
storage.protectionOnDesc: Your browser will keep Tasfer's local data safe from automatic cleanup.
storage.protectionUnsupportedDesc: This browser can't guarantee local data won't be cleaned up automatically. Export backups regularly.
sync.versionIncompatiblePeerOld: A device you're connected to is running an older version of Tasfer and can't sync until it's updated. Your local edits are unaffected.
sync.versionIncompatibleUpdate: A device you're connected to is running a newer version of Tasfer. Update the app to sync with it. Your local edits are unaffected.
sync.versionUpdateAvailableBody: A device you're connected to is running a newer version of Tasfer. Everything still syncs — update the app to get the latest features.
update.connectToInternet: Please connect to the internet to download the latest update and continue using the app.
update.newVersionAvailable: A new version of the app is available. Update now to get the latest features and improvements.
update.newVersionRequired: A new version of the app is required to continue. Please update to access the latest features and security improvements.

home.lp.eyebrow: a markdown editor that refuses to read your notes
home.lp.hero.titleA: Tasfer keeps every note on your machine.
home.lp.hero.lede1: Open a tab. Open a notes app. Type a half-formed idea, a draft email, a thing you would not say out loud. It lands on a server you do not own, in a city you have never been to, governed by a fourteen-page agreement you did not read.
home.lp.hero.metaTail: accounts · 0 trackers · you choose when to share
home.lp.premise.note: You would not mail your diary to a stranger. You type it into one, every morning, for free.
home.lp.premise.p1: You would not hand a notebook to someone in the street. You would not read your medical history into a stranger's phone. You would not shout your half-drafted resignation letter into a crowd.
home.lp.premise.p2: And yet — every free notes app, every free document, every free AI assistant is a trade. Your thoughts, in plain text, on a machine you will never see, indexed by software you will never read, owned by a company that will outlive you or be outlived by you. The bargain is so quiet you forget you took it.
home.lp.premise.pull: A grocery list deserves the same dignity as a manifesto.
home.lp.premise.p3a: Tasfer does not take the trade.
home.lp.premise.p3strong: Not the storage. Not the keys. Not the protocol. Not the permission to leave.
home.lp.mechanism.num: II — Where your work actually goes
home.lp.mechanism.p1: Every keystroke lands in your own storage, instantly — no round-trip, no "saving" spinner reporting back to a server somewhere. Tasfer works on a plane, in a basement, in a Faraday cage.
home.lp.mechanism.p2: When you sync, traffic flows peer-to-peer, encrypted with keys that never leave your hardware. Your laptop talks to your phone the way two friends meet — in person, with no third party in the room.
home.lp.mechanism.p3strong: Through a stateless relay — only when a direct path can't be made.
home.lp.mechanism.p3: It forwards bytes it cannot decrypt. No accounts. No logs. No memory of who connected to whom. It introduces, then forgets. And if you don't trust ours, point Tasfer at your own — it's a config field.
home.lp.pillar.local.name: local-first
home.lp.pillar.local.body: Every keystroke lands in your storage. Not 'syncs to your storage.' Lands. The network is a choice, not a dependency. Pull the plug and Tasfer keeps working.
home.lp.pillar.e2e.name: end-to-end encrypted
home.lp.pillar.e2e.body: Documents are encrypted on your device before they ever cross a wire. Only the people you share with hold the keys. The relay is structurally unable to read your work — not 'promises not to.' Cannot.
home.lp.pillar.relay.name: stateless relay
home.lp.pillar.relay.body: A postman with no inbox. It introduces peers, then forgets they met. No account database. No message log. No surface for a subpoena, a leak, or a curious engineer to land on.
home.lp.refusal.files.name: your files
home.lp.refusal.files.detail: Plain markdown. On your disk. In folders you arranged. Openable in any editor, today and in twenty years — long after we, and our company, are gone.
home.lp.refusal.keys.name: your keys
home.lp.refusal.keys.detail: End-to-end encryption with keys generated on your device. They never leave it. We could not read your notes if a court ordered us to.
home.lp.refusal.sync.name: your sync
home.lp.refusal.sync.detail: Direct, peer-to-peer, on your timing. Off by default. With the people you pick — and no one else in the room.
home.lp.refusal.backups.name: your backups
home.lp.refusal.backups.detail: Copy the folder. Email it to yourself. Print it. Bury it in a tin. Tasfer does not own a single byte you write.
home.lp.refusal.fork.name: your fork
home.lp.refusal.fork.detail: AGPL-3.0. Take the code. Swap the relay for your own. Ship your own build. The copyleft is the point: every fork inherits the same refusal.
home.lp.refusal.leave.name: your right to leave
home.lp.refusal.leave.detail: There is no account to delete because there was never one to begin with. You walk away by closing the tab.
home.lp.refusal.attention.name: your attention
home.lp.refusal.attention.detail: No ads. No analytics. No engagement loops. The page you are reading is the last one we will ever ask you to sit through.
home.lp.repo.p: Every privacy promise on the internet is a sentence on a webpage. Ours is a directory of source files. The encryption is in the repo. The relay is in the repo. The protocol is in the repo. Every line that runs between you and the words you wrote is something you can audit, fork, or replace with a build of your own.
home.lp.repo.repoSub: Every commit. Every dependency. Yours to read, yours to change, yours to keep.
home.lp.finale.sub: Install it. Fork it. Swap the relay. Take it offline for a year. Hand it to a friend. Bury it. Walk away. The choice was always supposed to be yours — Tasfer is the editor that finally agrees.
home.lp.footer.tagline: — decentralized by design, built in the open.
home.lp.relay.introduce.label: introduce
home.lp.relay.introduce.caption: The relay's only job: tell Alice and Bob how to reach each other. It does not authenticate them. It does not learn anything else.
home.lp.relay.direct.label: direct (default)
home.lp.relay.direct.caption: Alice and Bob talk peer-to-peer over an encrypted channel they alone negotiated. The relay is no longer in the path.
home.lp.relay.fallback.label: fallback
home.lp.relay.fallback.caption: When a direct connection isn't possible, the relay forwards encrypted bytes it cannot decrypt. It still holds no accounts and stores no messages.
onboarding.identityTitle: An identity was created for you.
onboarding.identityIntro: The moment you opened Tasfer, it generated a keypair on this device — that's your identity. The private key never leaves this machine, and there's no account or server behind it.
onboarding.bulletOnDevice: Everything stays on this device — until you choose to share.
onboarding.bulletNoAccount: No account, no cloud, no sign-up.
onboarding.bulletRecovery: Export a recovery file anytime to back it up.
onboarding.profileTitle: A face for sharing — if you want one.
onboarding.profileIntro: Tasfer works fully anonymous. The only time a name or avatar matters is when you invite someone to a space — it's how they'll tell your edits apart. You can add this now or never.
onboarding.spaceIntro: A space is a workspace that syncs directly between you and the people you invite — peer to peer, no server in the middle. Start one of your own, or join a peer's.
onboarding.createSpaceSub: Just for you. Invite others whenever you like.
onboarding.joinSpaceSub: Paste a code, import an invite, or scan a QR.
onboarding.createSpaceIntro: It lives only on this device until you invite someone. Give it a name to keep things organized — or leave it blank and it's simply yours.
onboarding.createSpaceNote: Nothing leaves your device until you generate an invite from inside the space.
onboarding.joinIntro: Use an invite from a peer. It hands your device the keys to sync directly with theirs.
onboarding.pasteCodePlaceholder: paste the base64 invite your peer sent you…
onboarding.scanHint: Point your camera at the QR code shown on your peer's device.
onboarding.scanDrawerHint: Hold your peer's QR code inside the frame. It connects the moment it reads.
onboarding.betaIntro: Access is limited to beta testers for now. Enter the access code.
onboarding.invalidAccessCode: That code doesn't match. Check it and try again.
manifest.description: Markdown notes that stay on your device — end-to-end encrypted, syncing directly between your devices. No cloud, no account.
